title: Gahnia arenaria
taxonomy: Gahnia arenaria is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family comprises a large group of monocotyledonous plants, including sedges and rushes. The genus Gahnia is further divided into several species, with Gahnia arenaria being one of them. The classification of Gahnia arenaria reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Cyperaceae family. This classification is used in botany to group plants based on their shared characteristics and genetic similarities.
uses: Gahnia arenaria, a plant native to Australia, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. It has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
